svg.text: Fit on the earlier program, tested on runs it never saw: the one-round law holds
14 supplier-removed em750 runs, 3 judge conditions, seeds 41-46 - axis unit: generated-candidate self-report score - frozen constants, no refitting
A. Factorization: does gap track rho x sigma?
predicted gap = rho x sigma (per round)
Base-judge points are the weak spot:
with only n=7 they scatter (r=0.52) and
B. Movement: does this round's gap set next round's drift?
realized kept-minus-pool gap at round r
The frozen law cuts drift error nearly
4x below assuming the pool just stays
Points: Panel A n=40 rounds (rho not null), Panel B n=42 round-to-round steps. Source: experiments/ablation_unit_law.json (scorer
analysis_spread_util_unified.py conventions). Frozen C=0.96, K=0.833 from simple_model_rollout.json / spread_util_unified.json.
